skill.name: setting-up-sending-domain
skill.description: Guides Mailtrap sending domain setup, DNS record publishing, verification troubleshooting, compliance steps, and click tracking preparation.
skill.summary: Set up and verify Mailtrap sending domains with DNS and API guidance.
skill.author: mailtrap

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command execution, network, and weak cryptography patterns, but most are false positives from Markdown code spans, documentation links, and .md URLs. The confirmed concerns are legitimate operational risks: the skill guides authenticated Mailtrap API usage, DNS provider automation, and DNS lookup commands.

medium_findings.title: Authenticated Mailtrap and DNS Provider Operations
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs use of bearer-token Mailtrap API requests and recommends creating DNS records through DNS provider APIs or infrastructure tooling. This is legitimate for the skill purpose, but incorrect automation can expose tokens, modify DNS, or affect email sending domain verification.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The API and DNS automation guidance is explicit and directly tied to the skill purpose. Risk depends on how the user supplies tokens and authorizes DNS changes.

medium_findings.title: DNS Lookup Commands Recommended
medium_findings.description: The skill recommends dig, nslookup, or an online DNS lookup to confirm public DNS records. These are standard diagnostic actions, but they can make external DNS queries and reveal the queried domain to the resolver or lookup service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command names are present in troubleshooting instructions and represent real external lookups. They are not evidence of hidden execution or malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Spans Misread as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged Markdown backticks as Ruby or shell execution. The cited lines contain inline examples such as hostnames, related skill names, environment variable placeholders, and API endpoint text, not executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review confirms these are Markdown inline code spans in documentation. No Ruby code, shell substitution, or dynamic command execution is present.

low_findings.title: Documentation Links Misread as Suspicious Network Calls
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs are user-facing Mailtrap documentation links, Mailtrap API endpoint examples, or DNS provider documentation links. No evidence found of hidden beaconing, data exfiltration, or automatic network requests.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs match the documented workflow and are visible to the user. The file contains no script that would call these URLs without user direction.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections do not correspond to cryptographic algorithm use. The cited lines are frontmatter text, documentation headings, provider guide text, a Markdown documentation link, and DNS proxy guidance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no MD5, SHA-1, weak cipher, or cryptographic API usage. The alert appears to be triggered by ordinary documentation text and .md links.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bearer Token API Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill references Authorization bearer tokens for Mailtrap API calls. This is required for the workflow, but users must avoid exposing tokens in logs, prompts, shared terminals, or generated scripts.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The token requirement is explicit. The skill does not include exfiltration behavior, but bearer tokens are sensitive credentials.

dangerous_patterns.title: DNS Record Automation Guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill suggests creating DNS records through DNS host APIs or infrastructure tooling. This is expected for automation, but it can change production DNS if used with privileged credentials.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The operational capability is clearly described. The risk is contextual and depends on permissions granted to DNS provider credentials.

content.value_statement: Sending from an unverified domain can delay email launches and create DNS confusion. This skill gives clear Mailtrap, DNS, API, and troubleshooting steps for domain verification.
actual_capabilities: Explains when a Mailtrap sending domain is required before live sending.
Guides users to add the exact root domain or subdomain used in From addresses.
Describes how to copy all Mailtrap DNS records into the active DNS provider.
References Mailtrap API endpoints for listing, creating, and checking sending domains.
Provides troubleshooting guidance for DNS propagation and proxied DNS records.
limitations: Does not create DNS records unless the user connects separate provider automation.
Does not include a full authentication workflow for obtaining Mailtrap tokens.
Cannot guarantee DNS propagation timing across all resolvers.
Does not replace Mailtrap compliance review or provider-specific policy checks.

use_cases.title: Prepare a New Sending Domain
use_cases.description: Set up DNS records for a new Mailtrap sending domain before production email sending begins.

use_cases.title: Troubleshoot Pending Verification
use_cases.description: Check whether Mailtrap DNS records are public and identify common propagation or proxy issues.

use_cases.title: Automate Domain Provisioning
use_cases.description: Use Mailtrap API guidance with DNS provider automation for repeatable domain onboarding.

prompt_templates.title: Start Domain Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me set up a Mailtrap sending domain for example.com. Explain each DNS step I should complete.

prompt_templates.title: Choose Root or Subdomain
prompt_templates.prompt: I send email from notifications.example.com. Tell me which domain I should add in Mailtrap and why.

prompt_templates.title: Diagnose Pending DNS
prompt_templates.prompt: My Mailtrap sending domain is still pending after I added DNS records. Walk me through verification checks.

prompt_templates.title: Plan API Automation
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a safe workflow to create a Mailtrap sending domain, fetch required DNS records, and publish them through a DNS provider API.

output_examples.input: I want to send from billing.example.com.
output_examples.output: Add billing.example.com as the Mailtrap sending domain. Copy every DNS record Mailtrap shows into the DNS zone that serves that subdomain.

output_examples.input: Mailtrap still says my domain is not verified.
output: Confirm each record is visible through public DNS lookup.
Check that names and values match Mailtrap exactly.
Disable DNS proxying for verification records unless Mailtrap documentation allows it.

output_examples.input: I need to provision many customer sending domains.
output_examples.output: Use the Mailtrap Sending Domains API to create or inspect domains, then publish returned DNS records through your DNS provider automation.

best_practices: Add every DNS record shown by Mailtrap, not only SPF or DKIM records.
Use the exact domain or subdomain that appears in the From address.
Keep Mailtrap API tokens and DNS provider credentials out of shared prompts and logs.
anti_patterns: Adding only the root domain when email sends from a subdomain.
Proxying verification CNAME records through a CDN when DNS-only records are required.
Clicking verify repeatedly before records are visible in public DNS.

faq.question: Do I need to verify a domain before sending live email?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill states that a domain you control must be added and verified before live sending.

faq.question: Should I add my root domain or a subdomain?
faq.answer: Add the exact hostname used in the From address. Use the subdomain if email sends from that subdomain.

faq.question: Can this skill publish DNS records for me?
faq.answer: It can guide API or provider automation, but it does not include built-in DNS publishing code.

faq.question: Why can verification remain pending?
faq.answer: Common causes include propagation delay, incorrect record names or values, missing records, or proxied verification records.

faq.question: Does the skill include Mailtrap API authentication details?
faq.answer: It references bearer token requirements and points to a related authorization skill for token handling.

faq.question: Which DNS providers are covered?
faq.answer: It references Mailtrap guides for Cloudflare, AWS Route 53, Google Cloud DNS, Squarespace, GoDaddy, Namecheap, and DigitalOcean.
